key_insight: A linearly implicit mass-lumped projection FEM can enforce the unit-sphere constraint exactly at mesh nodes while remaining unconditionally stable and optimally accurate for harmonic map flow.
engineering_relevance: This provides a robust, time-step-unrestricted finite element workflow for constrained nonlinear evolution problems, with clear implications for stable simulation of geometric PDEs in computational mechanics and multiphysics.
method: Predictor step uses a linearly implicit cross-product reformulation with mass-lumped FE discretization.
Apply nodal projection to restore exact unit-length at nodes; projected error contracts in discrete L2.
On tensor-product Cartesian meshes, the projection is also nonexpansive in discrete Dirichlet energy, yielding unconditional energy dissipation.
evidence_signal: The paper proves optimal a priori error bounds of O(Δt+h^2) in L∞(0,T;L2) and O(Δt+h) in L2(0,T;H1), and numerical experiments confirm both convergence rates and energy decay.
watch_item: The strongest discrete energy-stability result relies on Cartesian rectangular/cuboidal tensor-product meshes, so mesh choice and careful implementation of discrete inner products are important.
paper_title: Unconditional Optimal Error Estimates and Energy Stability for a Linearly Implicit Mass-Lumped Projection Finite Element Method for the Harmonic Map Flow
